Narrative_Meaning: Jacks signify youth, high spirits, parties, quarrels, protection, privilege and disagreements.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Tempest is the card of violent disturbance. People are scared, afraid, and otherwise perturbed by the violence of this situation, be it of the weather or a Character. Tempests can be tumultuous events of almost any sort, storms, wars, shipwrecks, political upheavals, coups, etc. It is a period of destruction, where the old is torn down, not necessarily to make way for the new.

Significator.Plot: The Tempest Plot is about destruction and tearing down the old world. Huge changes and shifts in social norms are indicated by the Tempest. It is the Plot of disaster movies, apocalypses and wars.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked by the Tempest things will not go well for it. The side may be destroyed, torn apart from within, but sometimes a new replacement Embodiment will appear within the chaos and lead what remains of this side as a stronger, changed version of the side.
Significator.Character:
The Tempest Significator indicates a Character who is violent and unpredictable.

They Gain Yin when they are attacked.

They Gain Yang when they attack.

They Gain Chi when they act randomly or react violently.

They also gain +1 Success Level on every attack.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A turbulent and volatile individual, whose actions are as unpredictable as a mounting storm. They thrive in the midst of conflict and upheaval, possessing a raw, destructive energy that makes them a formidable force when provoked.
Significator.Scene: The Tempest Scene is usually a survival Ordeal, caused by foul weather, it often throws unlikely people together as they try to survive the turmoil. Yarn-Tellers can play the card to evoke an enormous disaster that will usually befall their enemies, although they do occasionally get out of hand.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.
Yarn.Hook: It Was A Dark And Stormy Night...
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with inclement weather, this may reflect the Conflict in some strange way, or simply be a bad night to be out. Into this night is injected a scene of violence, perhaps a murder or assault. It might be the PCs who are attacked, or more likely the dying stranger at their door.
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: An artifact of immense power or a stable anchor in the flow of time—a monolith that stands firm amidst the swirling chaos.
Yarn.Fray_Description: A Tempest is a storm or similar huge event (Terrorist attack, aerial bombardment, war front, tsunami, volcano, etc) that uses 1-12 Force-Of-Nature Extras, or go for a Kaiju in extreme circumstances, to create an Extreme Stakes Motional Ordeal. These events are destructive to Locations / Stages which can result in paths being closed or creating new obstacles. Ideally, the PCs should have their own goals, entirely separate to the Tempest that they are trying to complete against this backdrop of destruction.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Ideally, one of the Embodiment's Allies, or a random passer-by, is disturbed by the actions, or sight of, of the PCs. This is especially common in bestial or ghostly Characters who didn't mean to scare the little girl... Alternatively, the Fray will be complicated by an alarm being triggered, usually by the PCs actions, this can include, Police Alarms, Fire Alarms, Security Alerts, Lock downs, or screams.

NPC.Description: A Prince is a noble ruler of an area, often brutal and inexperienced, they are usually defined by their doubts and how they deal with them.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: This Sweeping causes the PCs to think that their struggle is over, that they have won early. But this is just the Eye of the Storm, the storm will return. This is occasionally some attempt by the other sides to trick or flush out the PCs, but this should be used rarely and with care, more normally it is a chance to recover and rest, and perhaps a little preparation before the storm returns.
Yarn.History_Description: The Tempest indicates that the past leading to the current situation was destructive, attacks, fires and natural disasters like storms can wreck ships, destroy homes and relationships.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Tempest indicates that the current situation is a storm of some kind, often this is a natural event, as in a lightning or snow storm, but sometimes this may be a blitzkrieg, bombing campaign or invasion. The Tempest always indicates great danger and at least high stakes are in play.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Tempest indicates that the solution to the current situation is an act of violence. Of course, there is an ethical debate here, but in this situation violence will succeed.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about a danger that threatens the Character. This might be some imminent disaster, an ever-present threat or a distant concern.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information is about the violence that surrounds or is created by the Embodiment.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is carried by the atmosphere, this can be a scent on the wind, the rain or that thunder-like rumble could be a sign of a storm or the sound of distant guns.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is either about the Obstacles that stand in an Ordeal, or may be encoded as Obstacles in the next (or last) Ordeal. When they realise they were meant to count the number of spears in the pit trap. Oh, the look on their faces...
Revelations.Detail_Description: Confused details occur when some details never seem to be consistent, some witnesses say the dragon is red, others say it is black, the truth is there are two dragons in the swamp.

Obstacle.Description: It seems the Weather is not on your side and is hampering your progress, winds are against you, rain, hail, snow, even the sun, whatever really. The Weather is a (Vex / Chorus / Cast) Extra, depending on the Ref's mood and the Narrative (if you have been told about the storm rolling in for hours don't expect the a Vex).

Tarot.Normal: Target gains +2  Success Levels on any Social actions while atop the pile. Additionally adds a "Charm" Reserve for Target that they may "Prepare" Ordeal cards to for any social action.
Tarot.Crossed: Target takes +2 Failure Levels on any Social actions while atop the pile. Additionally automatically closes and discards any "Charm" Reserve.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 Obstacle in every Non-Court Stage, 2 in every Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: Jump over, past, or through an Obstacle. There's always at least one Obstacle that must be jumped over or through in this Stage.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: They swim, you swim after them.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Ebbing enemy are on their last legs, but they have decided to make a desperate fool-hardy assault with no advantages. If the Flowing forces survive the assault they will have won.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The opponent is a solitary Monster, usually well armoured (passive defences) and with strong attacks. Should be at least as powerful as any Two PCs. At least 2 ♣ Monster Facets
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc
Lea.Notes: Broccen may use Jack’s to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches, in this case specific Herbivores may be created. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.

Stress.Description: Stress can have profound effects on the mind, but real stress upsets the unconscious, making dreams become nightmares.
Stress.Rules: The Character’s current Stress and the current Tension Level are added together to create an amount of Chi. This Chi must be paid by the Character to side-step the Character partially into a Psycho-social space where they work through their stress confronting a series of nightmare scenarios that the Stresses represent. The Nightmares continue for a period of time equal to the Current Stress as Chi when the Character sleeps after which the nightmare will cease relieving 1 Stress. Typically the nightmares will reappear anytime the Character’s current Stress plus the Tension Level is high enough to create them again. Nightmares are typically an extension of the Character, so trying to harm them with magic, for example, will usually harm the Character experiencing the nightmares, of course sometimes Increated will use nightmares as the basis of a Nightmare Form, in which case they can be targeted.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can make a Character unbalanced, so that they can become emotionally tempestuous, as well as prone to outbursts of violence or loud outbursts such as screaming.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma makes the Character more emotional, erratic and prone to outbursts. When they initially take the Trauma all current Stress is Relieved instantly, creating a "Tempestuous Temper" Hitch which will interfere with the Character’s ability to control their emotions, perhaps even creating emotions, or instinctual responses. However the Character will be able to Relieve 1 Stress by using violence outbursts, whether physical or verbal, such as screaming, yelling, cursing, ect.

Age.Description: An Age of Chaos is occurring. During this age a society experiences upheavals, disturbances, tumults and uproar from within. Normal functioning within the society, such as trade, travel, leadership and communications are disrupted, usually this is not a complete disruption, but a limited period of disruption. Historically ages of chaos can include natural disasters, political upheavals, invasions, wars and similar historical events.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of great upheaval and disruption, where the old certainties of society are swept away by a whirlwind of change. It is an era of disturbances and tumult, where the breakdown of normal life leads to a period of intense uncertainty and struggle as people navigate the wreckage of the past.
Age.Rules: During the age of Chaos, everything changes, trade, communications and travel beyond and within the society is interrupted or confused somehow. Life within the society is more stressful and at higher Stakes than normal, during the age of Chaos.
